Details: Removal of the metolachlor degradates: metolachlor oxanilic acid (ME-OXA) and metolachlor sulphonic acid (ME-ESA) with PAC can be effective (23 to 98 percent removal) under a range of water quality parameters and PAC sorption conditions tested in bench-scale studies for two PACs and and four surface waters. From data presented in the bench-scale study, higher PAC dose and more contact time increased removal; depressing the pH or increasing the NOM concentration decreased removal. PAC showed greater removal of metolachlor in comparison to the removal of the metolachlor degradates. Monitoring at a full-scale plant showed 46 to 48 percent removal of the degradates by PAC alone, and 67 to 74 percent total removal of the degradates through a conventional treatment plant with GAC filters and PAC.
